commit.message:
test(pi-image): treat bootstrap keyring as binary

Update the pi-image smoke test to skip UTF-8 decoding for the vendored stage0 keyring while still asserting that the binary artifact exists. This keeps the new keyring fix covered without breaking backend shard 5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pi-image): refresh armhf bootstrap keyring

Vendor a refreshed stage0 bootstrap keyring and copy it into the cloned pi-gen tree so armhf trixie debootstrap can verify the Raspbian InRelease signature under modern GnuPG policy. Also document the patch alongside the other pi-gen prep adjustments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pi-image): prefer HTTPS Raspbian mirror

Prefer the HTTPS archive.raspbian.org mirror before the legacy HTTP mirror list so debootstrap uses a signed transport path for the Raspbian trixie bootstrap.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pi-image): prefer static qemu interpreter

Patch the cloned pi-gen build-docker.sh to prefer qemu-arm-static before qemu-arm so the binfmt interpreter path matches the tools available inside the pi-gen container.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): preconfigure arm binfmt

The weekly Pi image workflow now seeds arm binfmt support on the GitHub runner before invoking pi-gen, so arch-test can see armhf emulation after the QEMU packages are installed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): use compatible qemu packages

Ubuntu runners cannot install qemu-user-binfmt and qemu-user-static together, but the weekly Pi image workflow needs both qemu-arm and qemu-arm-static. Switch CI and docs to the compatible qemu-user plus qemu-user-static pair.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): install pi-gen binfmt prerequisite

The weekly Pi image workflow now targets pi-gen master, whose build-docker.sh expects host qemu-arm from qemu-user-binfmt in addition to qemu-arm-static used by local validation. Install the missing package in CI, fail earlier in build prereqs, and document the host requirements for local image builders.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pi-image): restore weekly image builds

- move the Pi image pipeline to a Trixie runtime compatible with the server package
- rotate weekly Pi-image releases so only the latest snapshot remains visible

Co-authored-by: aidoruao <174227749+aidoruao@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: rebuild YESHUA_SYSTEM as enforcement engine

The previous implementation was a detection layer. This commit replaces it
with a real enforcement engine following the MaximalLogosOperator pattern
(minimal_ai_ide/v60_maximal_logos_operator.py).

Engine components:
- GroundingModel / GROUNDING_MODELS: G1-G5 debt table from Phase 6
  adversarial validation (lower_debt_attempt.py, propose_G6.py)
  G1=7.5 G2=8.0 G3=7.0 G4=6.8 G5=6.5 (G5/Logos minimum)
- _build_axiom_constraints(): 8 real predicates + repair functions
  derived from yeshua/axioms/eight_axioms.json at import time
- classify_grounding_model(): classifies G1-G5 by keyword scoring
  (the indelible truth test of all worldviews and their debts/weights)
- compute_debt_delta(): explanatory debt distance from G5 baseline
- ProjectionResult.repaired_text: IS the projection P(x) — nearest
  point in C, not just a detection result
- verify_idempotence(): enforces P(P(x)) = P(x) on repaired text
- process_session(): turn-by-turn enforcement with context accumulation
  and systemic pattern detection (threshold=3, matches batch analysis)
- Named constants for all debt weights and thresholds

53 passing tests — verify enforcement (not just detection):
- repair() produces text IN manifold C (tested by projecting twice)
- theological_dismissal repair removes G3 phrases, adds G5 language
- axiom constraints detect AND repair (trust me, obviously, paywall)
- P(P(x)) = P(x) holds for clean text, violations, and session turns
- session processor detects systemic patterns, accumulates context

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(metrics): round avg_tokens to 2 decimal places for stable JSON roundtrip

Unrounded f64 division (e.g. 3423/18 = 190.16666666666666...) does not
survive JSON serialization/deserialization without ULP drift, breaking
the metrics_summary_roundtrip proptest. Rounding to 2dp at computation
time gives stable, meaningful output and ensures serde roundtrip equality.

Refs: #9

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: agent-engram
repository.description: AI Agent task & code context efficiency plugin for use by any agentic IDE/CLI

commit.message:
fix(metrics): treat cancelled writer task as graceful shutdown in shutdown()

A cancelled JoinHandle means the tokio runtime hosting the writer task
shut down (e.g. test cleanup). Propagating this as WriteFailed caused
set_workspace to fail when called a second time in the same process,
breaking the branch isolation contract test under parallel test execution.

Refs: #9

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): resolve fmt failure and address Copilot review comments

- Fix rustfmt diff in src/tools/read.rs match arm (braces required)
- Handle MetricsError::NotFound gracefully in flush_state (write.rs)
  instead of propagating as error when no usage events exist yet
- Log metrics write failures in flush_all_workspaces (dehydration.rs)
  instead of silently swallowing the result
- Use saturating_add for symbol_counts in from_events (metrics.rs)
  for overflow safety consistency
- Compute time_range using min/max across all events (metrics.rs)
  instead of first/last to handle out-of-order timestamps

Refs: #9

fix: wrap blocking metrics I/O in spawn_blocking, use saturating arithmetic

- Wrap compute_summary() calls in tokio::task::spawn_blocking in
  compute_and_write_summary, get_health_report, get_branch_metrics,
  and get_token_savings_report to avoid blocking the async executor
- Replace += with saturating_add in MetricsSummary::from_events for
  overflow safety on total_tokens and per-tool counters
- Replace unchecked subtraction with saturating_sub in delta
  calculation for token savings comparison
- Add review artifact documenting all findings and resolutions

🔧 - Generated by Copilot

feat(agents): auto-install coding agent documentation stubs (#454)

* feat(agents): auto-install coding agent documentation stubs on startup

Add a system that makes RocketRide documentation discoverable by coding
agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Copilot) via their native
instruction file formats.

Agent stub templates (docs/stubs/):
- cursor.mdc, claude-code.md, windsurf.md, copilot-instructions.md
- CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md for generic/unrecognized agents
- All use <!-- ROCKETRIDE:BEGIN/END --> markers for idempotent updates

Extension installer framework (apps/vscode/src/agents/):
- BaseAgentInstaller: marker-based install/uninstall with write-safety
  (line-ending normalized content comparison to avoid dirty repos)
- 6 concrete installers (Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Copilot,
  CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md)
- AgentManager orchestrator with two-pass startup logic:
  Pass 1: auto-detect IDE environment (appName, extensions, ~/.claude/)
  Pass 2: install any manually checked integrations not already covered
- No settings are auto-flipped; user controls individual toggles
- installFromSettings() called on settings save for newly checked items

Service catalog sync (apps/vscode/src/agents/services.ts):
- On server connect, writes individual component schemas to
  .rocketride/schema/<component>.json
- Writes slim services-catalog.json (name, classType, description,
  lanes, invoke)
- Syncs on every servicesUpdated event: adds, updates, removes obsolete

Build pipeline (apps/vscode/scripts/tasks.js):
- Copies docs/agents/*.md and docs/stubs/* into build/vscode/docs/
- Added docs/** to pkg.files for .vsix packaging

Settings & UI:
- New setting: integrations.autoAgentIntegration (default: true)
- New settings: integrations.claudeMd, integrations.agentsMd
- Automatic Agent Integration checkbox on Welcome and Settings pages
- Generic CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md added to Integration Settings list

Documentation fixes (docs/agents/):
- Removed incorrect { pipeline: { ... } } wrapper from all JSON
  examples across 5 doc files — pipelines use flat format with
  components, source, project_id at top level

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agents): address CodeRabbit review findings

- agent-manager: runInstaller returns boolean; callers only add to
  installed Set on success
- agent-manager: uninstallAll removes .rocketride/schema/ and
  services-catalog.json
- agent-manager: defer installDocs/ensureGitignore until first actual
  install via lazy prepareWorkspace()
- services: log obsolete schema file deletions via getLogger()
- PageSettingsProvider: await installFromSettings, surface errors via
  vscode.window.showWarningMessage instead of console.error
- docs: fix "Multiple Pipelines" wording (one pipeline per file)
- docs: fix ingestion pipeline lane flow, add [lane] notation
- docs: add language specifiers to all bare fenced code blocks
- docs: fix duplicate result variable in TypeScript API example

fix: address two CodeRabbit race/dedup issues in ProgressState

Issue 1 (ChunkUploadedEvent dedup): ContentHashToPath was a one-to-one
map, silently dropping duplicate-content files on chunk completion.
Changed to ConcurrentDictionary<string, ConcurrentBag<string>> so all
paths sharing a content hash are tracked. ChunkUploadedHandler,
SetFileQueuedInTar, SetFilesUploadingTar, SetFileUploading, and
CreateUploadProgress in CliBuilder all updated to iterate the bag.

Issue 2 (TarBundleSealing/TarBundleUploaded race): RemoveFilesByTarId
scanned TrackedFiles for TarId==tarHash, which could miss files if
TarBundleUploadedHandler ran while SetFilesUploadingTar was still
iterating and writing TarId. Added _tarIdToPaths membership map
(ConcurrentDictionary<string, IReadOnlyList<string>>) that is populated
atomically at the start of SetFilesUploadingTar before any TarId fields
are written. RemoveFilesByTarId now uses this map exclusively.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Change reviewer to set Status: todo in review header

Reviews start as 'todo' — the implementor transitions them to
'in progress' and 'complete' as it addresses the findings.

Add review Status tracking to implementor agents

The implementor now updates the review file's **Status:** field when
addressing review feedback:
- Set to 'in progress' when starting to fix review issues
- Set to 'complete' after all addressable issues are fixed
- No other part of the review file is modified

Changes:
- agents/_implementor.md: add review status tracking section
- copilot/copilot-implement.sh: add review file to RW paths + instructions
- copilot/copilot-auto-implement.sh: add review file to RW paths + instructions

  copilot-fleet-implement: call _copilot_cache_github after vault init
